findings.evidence: `ComoduleCat.category` is at lines 80-83, while the added composition lemmas are only for unbundled `Comodule.Hom.comp` at `Comodule/Hom.lean:128` and `:135`; existing category API already needed categorical restatements like `toLinearMap_comp` and `comp_apply` at `ComoduleCat.lean:155` and `:164`.
findings.fix: Add `ComoduleCat` restatements such as `toLinearMap_zero`, `toLinearMap_add`, `zero_apply`, `add_apply`, `(f + g) ≫ h`, `f ≫ (g + h)`, `0 ≫ f`, and `f ≫ 0`, with normal-form `[simp]` annotations.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat` gets the inherited `AddCommMonoid` on `M ⟶ N`, but no categorical `[simp]` API for addition/zero under `≫` or `toLinearMap`.

findings.evidence: `zero_toLinearMap` and `add_toLinearMap` are provided at lines 54 and 58, but the `nsmul_apply` and `sum_apply` API at lines 104 and 115 has no corresponding `toLinearMap` lemmas.
findings.fix: Add `[simp]` lemmas `(n • f).toLinearMap = n • f.toLinearMap` and `(∑ i ∈ s, f i).toLinearMap = ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).toLinearMap`.
findings.issue: `nsmul` and finite sums are only characterized by evaluation lemmas, unlike zero/add which also expose `toLinearMap` normal forms.

summary: The unbundled hom operations are present, but the public category API is incomplete for the stated additive hom-set target. Users working with `ComoduleCat` still lack the normal categorical restatements and linear-map characterizations for the new operations.
